etymology.notes: tuh tuh cf. Lane 319: a cry by which one chides the camel, and which makes him to run away

Preterit.turoyo: aḥna kulan mtahtáhle-lan b-i=briṯo 802; 57/120; naḥət w mlele qum ṣadre kefe w rahəṭ lak kalbe (...) mtahtahle ak kalbe
translations: uns hat er alle in alle Welt auseinandergetrieben
He went down and gathered some stones in front of his chest and ran towards the dogs. He dispersed the dogs.
